symptom: CUDA out of memory error occurs during training when batch size > 32
root_cause: GPU memory exceeded due to large batch size and model size
resolution: Reduce batch size, use gradient accumulation, or enable mixed precision (fp16)

symptom: CPU usage spikes to 100% while GPU remains mostly idle during training
root_cause: Data loading bottleneck due to insufficient parallel workers
resolution: Increase num_workers in DataLoader and enable prefetching/pinned memory

symptom: Training takes significantly longer than expected and GPU utilization is near 0%
root_cause: Model or tensors not moved to GPU
resolution: Ensure model and inputs are moved to CUDA device using .to(device)

symptom: Inference latency exceeds SLA requirements under normal load
root_cause: Model too large or not optimized for inference
resolution: Apply quantization, use smaller model, or enable batching for inference

symptom: Frequent out-of-memory crashes even with small batch sizes
root_cause: Memory leak due to retaining computation graphs or tensors
resolution: Use torch.no_grad during inference and delete unused tensors explicitly

symptom: GPU utilization remains below 30% during training
root_cause: Slow data pipeline causing GPU starvation
resolution: Optimize data loading, use caching, and increase DataLoader workers

symptom: Training slows down significantly when reading large datasets
root_cause: Frequent disk reads without caching or batching
resolution: Cache dataset in memory or use efficient data formats like Parquet

symptom: Training job gets killed without error logs in cluster environment
root_cause: Exceeded memory or compute quota limits
resolution: Check resource quotas and request higher limits or reduce resource usage

symptom: Model loading takes several minutes during service startup
root_cause: Large model size and slow disk I/O
resolution: Use model compression, lazy loading, or load model into memory once

symptom: Inference API experiences sudden latency spikes under load
root_cause: Network congestion or insufficient request handling capacity
resolution: Use load balancing and autoscaling to distribute traffic

symptom: Autoscaling does not trigger even when CPU usage is high
root_cause: Incorrect scaling thresholds or misconfigured metrics
resolution: Verify autoscaling policies and adjust trigger thresholds

symptom: Containers restart frequently with OOMKilled status
root_cause: Memory limits too low for workload requirements
resolution: Increase memory limits and optimize memory usage

symptom: Requests queue up and response times increase significantly
root_cause: Insufficient worker processes handling requests
resolution: Increase number of workers or scale horizontally

symptom: Memory usage increases over time leading to fragmentation
root_cause: Frequent allocation and deallocation of tensors
resolution: Reuse memory buffers and optimize allocation patterns

symptom: GPU temperature rises above safe limits during prolonged training
root_cause: Sustained high utilization without adequate cooling
resolution: Improve cooling, reduce workload, or monitor thermal throttling

symptom: Checkpoint saving takes excessive time and slows training loop
root_cause: Slow disk write speed or large checkpoint size
resolution: Use faster storage (SSD) and save checkpoints less frequently

symptom: Distributed training job fails intermittently across nodes
root_cause: Node failures or unstable cluster environment
resolution: Check node health and ensure cluster stability

symptom: Training speed decreases as number of nodes increases
root_cause: Communication overhead dominates computation
resolution: Optimize communication backend and reduce synchronization frequency

symptom: Cluster resources remain idle despite queued jobs
root_cause: Inefficient job scheduling or resource fragmentation
resolution: Tune scheduler configuration and improve resource allocation strategy

symptom: Frequent request timeouts in API under moderate load
root_cause: Slow model inference or blocking operations
resolution: Optimize inference pipeline and use asynchronous request handling

symptom: Data transfer between nodes is significantly slower than expected
root_cause: Network bandwidth limitations or misconfigured networking
resolution: Upgrade network infrastructure or optimize data transfer patterns

symptom: High CPU wait time observed in monitoring tools
root_cause: Processes blocked waiting for I/O operations
resolution: Use asynchronous I/O or batch disk operations

symptom: Multiple jobs interfere with each other causing slowdowns
root_cause: Resource contention between workloads
resolution: Isolate workloads using containers or dedicated resources

symptom: Throughput remains low despite sufficient hardware resources
root_cause: Single-threaded execution limiting performance
resolution: Parallelize computation using multi-threading or distributed processing

symptom: Service startup time is very slow after deployment
root_cause: Heavy initialization and loading of large dependencies
resolution: Use lazy loading and pre-initialize critical components

symptom: Cache hit rate is low leading to repeated expensive computations
root_cause: Inefficient caching strategy or poor key design
resolution: Optimize cache keys and caching policy

symptom: GPU remains idle between training steps
root_cause: Inefficient pipeline scheduling or synchronization delays
resolution: Overlap computation and data loading using asynchronous pipelines

symptom: Application crashes due to disk full errors
root_cause: Logs or temporary files accumulating without cleanup
resolution: Implement log rotation and periodic cleanup jobs

symptom: Container build times are excessively long
root_cause: Large dependencies and inefficient Dockerfile layering
resolution: Use multi-stage builds and minimize dependencies

symptom: Distributed training jobs fail due to communication errors
root_cause: Misconfigured network settings or firewall restrictions
resolution: Verify network configuration and ensure proper node communication
